setting.note: Yemen in the 1940s, under the rule of the Imamate. A boy from a highland tribe is held as a hostage for his family's loyalty and made a page-servant in the governor's fortress. The map runs from the mountain city of Taiz below Jabal Sabir north across the highlands to the imamic capital at Sana'a, roughly 250 kilometres away.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Zayd Mutee Dammaj, first published in Arabic as Ar-Rahina, 1984. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Hostage, Zayd Mutee Dammaj (1984). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The rust-red of the highland stone and the fortress walls above Taiz. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
